epjyd6m0"><div class="css-1baulvz"><p class="css-1nuro5j e1jsehar1"><span class="byline-prefix">By </span><a href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/by/david-gelles" class="css-brehiz e1jsehar0"><span class="css-1baulvz">David Gelles</span></a>, <a href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/by/james-b-stewart" class="css-brehiz e1jsehar0"><span class="css-1baulvz">James B. Stewart</span></a>, <a href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/by/jessica-silver-greenberg" class="css-brehiz e1jsehar0"><span class="css-1baulvz">Jessica Silver-Greenberg</span></a> and <a href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/by/kate-kelly" class="css-brehiz e1jsehar0"><span class="css-1baulvz last-byline">Kate Kelly</span></a></p></div></div><ul class="css-1u1psjv epjyd6m3"><li class="css-ccw2r3 epjyd6m1"><time class="css-129k401 e16638kd0" datetime="2018-08-16T23:22:11-04:00">Aug. 16, 2018</time></li></ul></div></div></header><section name="articleBody" class="meteredContent css-1r7ky0e"><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0"><a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2019/07/16/technology/neuralink-elon-musk.html" title="">Elon Musk</a> was at home in Los Angeles, struggling to maintain his composure. “This past year has been the most difficult and painful year of my career,” he said. “It was excruciating.”</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">The year has only gotten more intense for Mr. Musk, the chairman and chief executive of the electric-car maker Tesla, since he abruptly <a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/08/07/business/tesla-stock-elon-musk-private.html" title="">declared on Twitter</a> last week that he hoped to convert the publicly traded company into a private one. The episode kicked off a furor in the markets and within Tesla itself, and he acknowledged on Thursday that he was fraying.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">In an hourlong interview with The New York Times, he choked up multiple times, noting that he nearly missed his brother’s wedding this summer and spent his birthday holed up in Tesla’s offices as the company raced to meet elusive production targets on a crucial new model.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Asked if the exhaustion was taking a toll on his physical health, Mr. Musk answered: “It’s not been great, actually. I’ve had friends come by who are really concerned.”</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0"><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">[</em><a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/08/17/business/azealia-banks-elon-musk.html" title=""><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">Read about the bizarre subplot</em></a><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0"> involving Mr. Musk and the rapper Azealia Banks.]</em></p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">The events set in motion by Mr. Musk’s tweet have ignited a <a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/08/15/business/tesla-musk-sec-subpoena-goldman.html" title="">federal investigation</a> and have angered some board members, according to people familiar with the matter. Efforts are underway to find a No. 2 executive to help take some of the pressure off Mr. Musk, people briefed on the search said. And some board members have expressed concern not only about Mr. Musk’s workload but also about his use of Ambien, two people familiar with the board said.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">For two decades, Mr. Musk has been one of Silicon Valley’s most brash and ambitious entrepreneurs, helping to found several influential technology companies. He has often carried himself with bravado, dismissing critics and relishing the spotlight that has come with his success and fortune. But in the interview, he demonstrated an extraordinary level of self-reflection and vulnerability, acknowledging that his myriad executive responsibilities are taking a steep personal toll.</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><figure class="sizeLarge css-qhlm6o" aria-label="media" role="group"><div class="css-bsn42l"><span class="css-1dv1kvn">Video</span><div><div class="css-n27z15" style="padding-bottom:56.25%"><div class="css-mm3pwi"><div class="css-1g7y0i5 e1drnplw0"><button tabindex="100" class="css-1rtlxy" type="button" aria-label="close"><svg width="60" height="60" viewbox="0 0 60 60" fill="none"><circle cx="30" cy="30" r="30" fill="white" fill-opacity="0.9"></circle><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M38.4844 20.1006L39.8986 21.5148L21.5138 39.8996L20.0996 38.4854L38.4844 20.1006Z" fill="black"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M21.5156 20.1006L20.1014 21.5148L38.4862 39.8996L39.9004 38.4854L21.5156 20.1006Z" fill="black"></path></svg></button><div class="css-1ceswkc e1drnplw1"></div><div class="css-f2fzwx e1drnplw2"><div aria-labelledby="modal-title" role="region"><header class="css-jx2kos"><div class="css-mln36k" id="modal-title">transcript</div><button type="button" class="css-18p0uq6"><div class="css-pbq7ev"></div><span>Back</span></button><div class="css-f6lhej"><div class="css-1ialerq"><button tabindex="99" type="button" class="css-1eut9gw" aria-label="play"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" viewbox="0 0 20 20" fill="none"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M8 13.7683V6L14.5 9.88415L8 13.7683Z" fill="#333333"></path><circle cx="10" cy="10" r="9.25" stroke="#333333" stroke-width="1.5"></circle></svg></button><div class="css-1701swk"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewbox="0 0 40 36" id="el_0kpS9qL_S"><title>bars</title><g id="el_oZ84Hna1GC_65hRV2Qwn" class="css-1fxvzwo" data-animator-group="true" data-animator-type="0"><g id="el_oZ84Hna1GC_ILVvi2tqx" class="css-1wnday1" ata-animator-group="true" data-animator-type="2"><g id="el_oZ84Hna1GC"><rect x="34" width="6" height="36" id="el_qw_T_tngXw"></rect></g></g></g><g id="el_mYVjkduhMU_p_9Pm85Ac" class="css-fwki7z" data-animator-group="true" data-animator-type="0"><g id="el_mYVjkduhMU_WxG3R40yd" class="css-t3i5e6" data-animator-group="true" data-animator-type="2"><g id="el_mYVjkduhMU"><rect x="22.67" width="6" height="36" id="el_lf9GrROk6j"></rect></g></g></g><g id="el_o-EuxhgoAw_kYNRGDfcw" class="css-t9te0w" data-animator-group="true" data-animator-type="0"><g id="el_o-EuxhgoAw_3c3bzSjOJ" class="css-1r5375t" ata-animator-group="true" data-animator-type="2"><g id="el_o-EuxhgoAw"><rect x="11.33" width="6" height="36" id="el_-iueO8klO0"></rect></g></g></g><g id="el_F7mSMPhqpC_y_fKcpSxn" class="css-qknaag" data-animator-group="true" data-animator-type="0"><g id="el_F7mSMPhqpC_R6bNB6_Ys" class="css-1ruvd04" ata-animator-group="true" data-animator-type="2"><g id="el_F7mSMPhqpC"><rect width="6" height="36" id="el_dS5TKNZZ5w"></rect></g></g></g></svg></div><div><div class="css-1t7yl1y">0:00<!-- -->/<!-- -->3:32</div><div class="css-og85jy">-<!-- -->0:00</div></div></div></div></header><div class="css-15fbio0"><div class="css-1p4nyns"><p class="css-1qi8px4">transcript</p><h2 class="css-9wqu2x">Elon Musk’s Highs and Lows: PayPal, SpaceX, Tesla</h2><h4 class="css-qsd3hm">Serial entrepreneur Elon Musk wants to fundamentally change the way we live. But his path to success has been characterized by both great accomplishments and flirtations with failure.</h4></div><dl class="css-p98d0w"><dt class="css-xx7kwh"></dt><dd class="css-12tddc8"><p class="css-8hvvyd">“I thought about what are the problems that are most likely to affect the future of the world or future of humanity.” Elon Musk, a South African-born engineer, inventor and billionaire, is one of the most important entrepreneurs in the world. But the 47-year-old is no stranger to turmoil, and by his own account his career has been full of highs and lows. “Just three years ago, I was showering at the Y and sleeping on the office floor. And now, I have got a million-dollar car and quite a few creature comforts.” After making a fortune with his first successful startup, Zip2, Musk gained fame as the co-founder of PayPal in the late 1990s. “So this is an ATM. What we’re going to do is transform the traditional banking industry.” The company lost hundreds of millions of dollars, and Musk was ousted as CEO because of internal turmoil. But when PayPal went public in 2002, and then was sold to eBay, Musk — as the biggest shareholder — walked away with $200 million. By the mid-2000s, Musk had turned around and invested almost all of his PayPal fortune into his new companies: SpaceX, Tesla and SolarCity. With SpaceX, Musk’s aspiration is out of this world — literally. It’s a commercial space flight company, which designs and launches advanced rockets into space. “Behind me is where Falcon 9 would sit during launch.” One of its ambitions is to eventually send people to Mars. “And I can’t think of anything more exciting than going out there and being among the stars.” But it has faced some roadblocks. Their first three rocket launches — failed, burning through almost all of the company’s assets. But SpaceX has had recent successes. The Falcon Heavy launch earlier this year was a big milestone and it carried an interesting payload: a dummy astronaut riding a Tesla car that was orbiting space. The company has also landed contracts with NASA, but questions about safety and cost remain. “We sold over 1,000 cars — and by sold I mean people put down a substantial deposit on the car.” Tesla, Musk’s electric car company, is one of the first of its kind to work on a large scale. It has also successfully disrupted the auto industry. The company is valued at over $50 billion. The Tesla Model S has been called the greatest car ever built. But Tesla has also faced some setbacks, including car batteries that caught on fire, recalls and production delays. SolarCity, Musk’s renewable energy venture, initially had a bright future. It’s the largest provider of rooftop solar systems in the U.S., and the company boomed on Wall Street. But after investors raised doubts about its business model, SolarCity’s shares quickly lost value, and Tesla had to take over in what was widely seen as a bailout. “We really need to make solar panels as appealing as electric cars have become.” But while Musk may have been flirting with bankruptcy and calamity for years, his projects continue to captivate the collective imagination. “Life has to be more than about solving problems. There have to be things that make you proud to be a member of humanity.”</p></dd></dl></div></div></div></div><script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/http://schema.org","@type":"VideoObject","@id":"http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/video/business/100000006060092/elon-musk-tesla-spacex.html","description":"Serial entrepreneur Elon Musk wants to fundamentally change the way we live. But his path to success has been characterized by both great accomplishments and flirtations with failure.","url":"http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/video/business/100000006060092/elon-musk-tesla-spacex.html","name":"Elon Musk’s Highs and Lows: PayPal, SpaceX, Tesla","thumbnailUrl":["http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://static01.nyt.com/images/2018/08/22/us/19xp-musk-vid-2/19xp-musk-vid-2-videoSixteenByNine1050.jpg","http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://static01.nyt.com/images/2018/08/22/us/19xp-musk-vid-2/merlin_133348488_f63a7f63-ad9c-4f6c-91fd-2994b1df80f0-superJumbo.jpg","http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://static01.nyt.com/images/2018/08/22/us/19xp-musk-vid-2/19xp-musk-vid-2-square640.jpg"],"embedUrl":"http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/video/players/offsite/index.html?videoId=100000006060092","uploadDate":"2018-08-18T19:55:38.000Z","transcript":"“I thought about what are the problems that are most likely to affect the future of the world or future of humanity.” Elon Musk, a South African-born engineer, inventor and billionaire, is one of the most important entrepreneurs in the world. But the 47-year-old is no stranger to turmoil, and by his own account his career has been full of highs and lows. “Just three years ago, I was showering at the Y and sleeping on the office floor. And now, I have got a million-dollar car and quite a few creature comforts.” After making a fortune with his first successful startup, Zip2, Musk gained fame as the co-founder of PayPal in the late 1990s. “So this is an ATM. What we’re going to do is transform the traditional banking industry.” The company lost hundreds of millions of dollars, and Musk was ousted as CEO because of internal turmoil. But when PayPal went public in 2002, and then was sold to eBay, Musk — as the biggest shareholder — walked away with $200 million. By the mid-2000s, Musk had turned around and invested almost all of his PayPal fortune into his new companies: SpaceX, Tesla and SolarCity. With SpaceX, Musk’s aspiration is out of this world — literally. It’s a commercial space flight company, which designs and launches advanced rockets into space. “Behind me is where Falcon 9 would sit during launch.” One of its ambitions is to eventually send people to Mars. “And I can’t think of anything more exciting than going out there and being among the stars.” But it has faced some roadblocks. Their first three rocket launches — failed, burning through almost all of the company’s assets. But SpaceX has had recent successes. The Falcon Heavy launch earlier this year was a big milestone and it carried an interesting payload: a dummy astronaut riding a Tesla car that was orbiting space. The company has also landed contracts with NASA, but questions about safety and cost remain. “We sold over 1,000 cars — and by sold I mean people put down a substantial deposit on the car.” Tesla, Musk’s electric car company, is one of the first of its kind to work on a large scale. It has also successfully disrupted the auto industry. The company is valued at over $50 billion. The Tesla Model S has been called the greatest car ever built. But Tesla has also faced some setbacks, including car batteries that caught on fire, recalls and production delays. SolarCity, Musk’s renewable energy venture, initially had a bright future. It’s the largest provider of rooftop solar systems in the U.S., and the company boomed on Wall Street. But after investors raised doubts about its business model, SolarCity’s shares quickly lost value, and Tesla had to take over in what was widely seen as a bailout. “We really need to make solar panels as appealing as electric cars have become.” But while Musk may have been flirting with bankruptcy and calamity for years, his projects continue to captivate the collective imagination. “Life has to be more than about solving problems. There have to be things that make you proud to be a member of humanity.”","duration":"PT3M31.411S"}</script></div><div class="css-1cueeje" style="padding-bottom:56.25%;transition:opacity 300ms ease-in-out"><div class="css-1ihorw"><img src="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455im_/https://static01.nyt.com/images/2018/08/22/us/19xp-musk-vid-2/19xp-musk-vid-2-videoSixteenByNine3000.jpg" alt="Video player loading"/></div><div class="css-1ruigs3"><div class="css-v15h5m"><div></div><div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><figcaption class="css-17ai7jg e18f7pbr0"><span class="css-16f3y1r e13ogyst0">Serial entrepreneur Elon Musk wants to fundamentally change the way we live. But his path to success has been characterized by both great accomplishments and flirtations with failure.</span><span class="css-cch8ym"><span class="css-1dv1kvn">Credit</span><span class="css-cnj6d5 e1z0qqy90"><span class="css-1ly73wi e1tej78p0">Credit...</span><span>Todd Anderson for The New York Times</span></span></span></figcaption></figure><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">In the interview, Mr. Musk provided a detailed timeline of the events leading up to the Twitter postings on Aug. 7 in which he said he was considering taking the company private at $420 a share. He asserted that he had “funding secured” for such a deal — a transaction likely to be worth well over $10 billion.</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0"><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">[</em><a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/08/17/business/tesla-elon-musk-interview-takeaways.html" title=""><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">Read the five key takeaways</em></a><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0"> from our interview with Elon Musk.]</em></p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">That morning, Mr. Musk woke up at home with his girlfriend, the musician known as Grimes, and had an early workout. Then he got in a Tesla Model S and drove himself to the airport. En route, Mr. Musk typed his fateful message.</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-cfo9c3"></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk has said he saw the tweet as an attempt at transparency. He acknowledged Thursday that no one had seen or reviewed it before he posted it.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Tesla’s shares soared. Investors, analysts and journalists puzzled over the tweet — published in the middle of the day’s official market trading, an unusual time to release major news — including the price Mr. Musk cited. He said in the interview that he wanted to offer a roughly 20 percent premium over where the stock had been recently trading, which would have been about $419. He decided to round up to $420 — a number that has become code for marijuana in counterculture lore.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">“It seemed like better karma at $420 than at $419,” he said in the interview. “But I was not on weed, to be clear. Weed is not helpful for productivity. There’s a reason for the word ‘stoned.’ You just sit there like a stone on weed.”</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk reached the airport and flew on a private plane to Nevada, where he spent the day visiting a Tesla battery plant known as the Gigafactory, including time meeting with managers and working on an assembly line. That evening, he flew to the San Francisco Bay Area, where he held Tesla meetings late into the night.</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-79elbk" data-testid="photoviewer-wrapper"><div class="css-z3e15g" data-testid="photoviewer-wrapper-hidden"></div><div data-testid="photoviewer-children" class="css-1a48zt4 ehw59r15"><figure class="css-jcw7oy e1g7ppur0" aria-label="media" role="group"><div class="css-1xdhyk6 erfvjey0"><span class="css-1ly73wi e1tej78p0">Image</span><img alt="Mr. Musk declared on Twitter last week that he hoped to convert the publicly traded company into a private one. Tesla’s shares soared afterward." class="css-1m50asq" src="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455im_/https://static01.nyt.com/images/2018/08/17/business/17MUSK05/merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-articleLarge.jpg?quality=75&amp;auto=webp&amp;disable=upscale" srcset="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455im_/https://static01.nyt.com/images/2018/08/17/business/17MUSK05/merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-articleLarge.jpg?quality=90&amp;auto=webp 600w, http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455im_/https://static01.nyt.com/images/2018/08/17/business/17MUSK05/merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-jumbo.jpg?quality=90&amp;auto=webp 1024w, http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455im_/https://static01.nyt.com/images/2018/08/17/business/17MUSK05/merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-superJumbo.jpg?quality=90&amp;auto=webp 2048w" sizes="((min-width: 600px) and (max-width: 1004px)) 84vw, (min-width: 1005px) 60vw, 100vw" decoding="async"/></div><figcaption class="css-1l44abu ewdxa0s0"><span aria-hidden="true" class="css-16f3y1r e13ogyst0">Mr. Musk declared on Twitter last week that he hoped to convert the publicly traded company into a private one. Tesla’s shares soared afterward.</span><span class="css-cnj6d5 e1z0qqy90"><span class="css-1ly73wi e1tej78p0">Credit...</span><span>David Paul Morris/Bloomberg</span></span></figcaption></figure></div></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">What Mr. Musk meant by “funding secured” has become an important question. Those two words helped propel Tesla’s shares higher.</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">But that funding, it turned out, was far from secure.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk has said he was referring to a potential investment by Saudi Arabia’s government investment fund. Mr. Musk had extensive talks with representatives of the $250 billion fund about possibly financing a transaction to take Tesla private — maybe even in a manner that would have resulted in the Saudis’ owning most of the company. One of those sessions took place on July 31 at the Tesla factory in the Bay Area, according to a person familiar with the meeting. But the Saudi fund had not committed to provide any cash, two people briefed on the discussions said.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Another possibility under consideration is that SpaceX, Mr. Musk’s rocket company, would help bankroll the Tesla privatization and would take an ownership stake in the carmaker, according to people familiar with the matter.</p><div role="complementary" aria-labelledby="storyline-latest-updates" class="css-y1f5ai"><div class="css-8atqhb"><div class="css-nl7gl3">Business &amp; Economy</div><h2 id="storyline-latest-updates" class="css-5o8lnh"><a href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/live/2020/12/23/business/us-economy-coronavirus">Latest Updates</a></h2><div class="css-rchdvv"><time class="css-101kej7" datetime="2020-12-23T13:59:03.202Z"><span>Updated </span><div class="css-ki347z"><span class="css-1stvlmo">Dec. 23, 2020, 8:59 a.m. ET</span><span class="css-kpxlkr"></span></div></time><span class="css-1dv1kvn" aria-live="polite"></span></div><ul class="css-15zvb7e"><li class="css-6exgss"><a href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/live/2020/12/23/business/us-economy-coronavirus#extension-of-federal-jobless-benefits-may-not-prevent-a-brief-lapse">Extension of federal jobless benefits may not prevent a brief lapse.</a></li><li class="css-6exgss"><a href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/live/2020/12/23/business/us-economy-coronavirus#frustration-rises-at-britains-ports-over-clearing-a-logjam-of-thousands-of-trucks">Frustration rises at Britain’s ports over clearing a logjam of thousands of trucks.</a></li><li class="css-6exgss"><a href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/live/2020/12/23/business/us-economy-coronavirus#stimulus-snap">How the aid bill changes the food stamp program.</a></li></ul><div class="css-gczbks styln-survey-component" id="storyline-survey-latest-updates"></div></div></div><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk’s tweet kicked off a chain reaction.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">An hour and 20 minutes after the tweet, with Tesla’s shares up 7 percent, the Nasdaq stock exchange halted trading, and Tesla published a letter to employees from Mr. Musk explaining the rationale for possibly taking the company private. When the shares resumed trading, they continued their climb, ending the day with an 11 percent gain.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">The next day, investigators in the San Francisco office of the Securities and Exchange Commission asked Tesla for explanations. Ordinarily, such material information about a public company’s plans is laid out in detail after extensive internal preparation and issued through official channels. Board members, blindsided by the chief executive’s market-moving statement, were angry that they had not been briefed, two people familiar with the matter said. They scrambled to cobble together a public statement trying to defuse a mounting uproar over the seemingly haphazard communication.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk said in the interview that board members had not complained to him about his tweet. “I don’t recall getting any communications from the board at all,” he said. “I definitely did not get calls from irate directors.”</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-79elbk" data-testid="photoviewer-wrapper"><div class="css-z3e15g" data-testid="photoviewer-wrapper-hidden"></div><div data-testid="photoviewer-children" class="css-1a48zt4 ehw59r15"><figure class="css-jcw7oy e1g7ppur0" aria-label="media" role="group"><div class="css-1xdhyk6 erfvjey0"><span class="css-1ly73wi e1tej78p0">Image</span><div class="css-zjzyr8"><div data-testid="lazyimage-container" style="height:299.02222222222224px"></div></div></div><figcaption class="css-1l44abu ewdxa0s0"><span aria-hidden="true" class="css-16f3y1r e13ogyst0">The Model 3 assembly line at Tesla&rsquo;s factory in Fremont, Calif. The company has been racing to meet production targets on the new model.</span><span class="css-cnj6d5 e1z0qqy90"><span class="css-1ly73wi e1tej78p0">Credit...</span><span>Justin Kaneps for The New York Times</span></span></figcaption></figure></div></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">But shortly after the Times published its interview with Mr. Musk, he added through a Tesla spokeswoman that Antonio Gracias, Tesla’s lead independent director, had indeed contacted him to discuss the Aug. 7 Twitter post, and that he had agreed not to tweet again about the possible privatization deal unless he had discussed it with the board.</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">In the interview, Mr. Musk added that he did not regret his Twitter post — “Why would I?” — and said he had no plans to stop using the social media platform. Some board members, however, have recently told Mr. Musk that he should lay off Twitter and focus on making cars and launching rockets, according to people familiar with the matter.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0"><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">[</em><a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/08/16/opinion/elon-musk-crazy-tesla.html" title=""><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">Kara Swisher</em></a><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">, a Times opinion columnist, writes that Mr. Musk is “deeply human, with all the positive and negative characteristics that suggests.”]</em></p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">The S.E.C. investigation appears to be intensifying rapidly. Just days after the agency’s request for information, Tesla’s board and Mr. Musk received S.E.C. subpoenas, according to a person familiar with the matter. Board members and Mr. Musk are preparing to meet with S.E.C. officials as soon as next week, the person said.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">In the interview on Thursday, Mr. Musk alternated between laughter and tears.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">He said he had been working up to 120 hours a week recently — echoing the reason he cited in a recent public apology to an analyst whom he had berated. In the interview, Mr. Musk said he had not taken more than a week off since 2001, when he was bedridden with malaria.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">“There were times when I didn’t leave the factory for three or four days — days when I didn’t go outside,” he said. “This has really come at the expense of seeing my kids. And seeing friends.”</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-79elbk" data-testid="photoviewer-wrapper"><div class="css-z3e15g" data-testid="photoviewer-wrapper-hidden"></div><div data-testid="photoviewer-children" class="css-1a48zt4 ehw59r15"><figure class="css-jcw7oy e1g7ppur0" aria-label="media" role="group"><div class="css-1xdhyk6 erfvjey0"><span class="css-1ly73wi e1tej78p0">Image</span><div class="css-zjzyr8"><div data-testid="lazyimage-container" style="height:264.8666666666667px"></div></div></div><figcaption class="css-1l44abu ewdxa0s0"><span aria-hidden="true" class="css-16f3y1r e13ogyst0">The launch of the SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket at Kennedy Space Center in February. SpaceX, Mr. Musk’s rocket company, is said to be a possible source of funding for the Tesla privatization.</span><span class="css-cnj6d5 e1z0qqy90"><span class="css-1ly73wi e1tej78p0">Credit...</span><span>Joe Burbank/Orlando Sentinel, via Associated Press</span></span></figcaption></figure></div></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk stopped talking, seemingly overcome by emotion.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">He turned 47 on June 28, and he said he spent the full 24 hours of his birthday at work. “All night — no friends, nothing,” he said, struggling to get the words out.</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Two days later, he was scheduled to be the best man at the wedding of his brother, Kimbal, in Catalonia. Mr. Musk said he flew directly there from the factory, arriving just two hours before the ceremony. Immediately afterward, he got back on the plane and returned straight to Tesla headquarters, where work on the mass-market Model 3 has been all consuming.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk paused again.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">“I thought the worst of it was over — I thought it was,” he said. “The worst is over from a Tesla operational standpoint.” He continued: “But from a personal pain standpoint, the worst is yet to come.”</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">He blamed short-sellers — investors who bet that Tesla’s shares will lose value — for much of his stress. He said he was bracing for “at least a few months of extreme torture from the short-sellers, who are desperately pushing a narrative that will possibly result in Tesla’s destruction.”</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Referring to the short-sellers, he added: “They’re not dumb guys, but they’re not supersmart. They’re O.K. They’re smartish.”</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0"><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">[</em><a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/08/17/business/dealbook/elon-musk-tesla-short-stock.html" title=""><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">Short-sellers made $1 billion</em></a><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0"> as Tesla stock dropped 9 percent on Friday.]</em></p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk’s tweets on Aug. 7 were the most recent of several flare-ups that had drawn scrutiny. He wrangled with short-sellers and belittled analysts for asking “<a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/05/03/business/tesla-elon-musk.html" title="">boring, bonehead</a>” questions. And after sending a team of engineers from one of his companies to help rescue members of a stranded soccer team, he lashed out at a cave diver who was dismissive of the gesture, deriding him on Twitter as a “<a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/07/18/business/elon-musk-vern-unsworth-pedo-guy.html" title="">pedo guy</a>,” or pedophile.</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-79elbk" data-testid="photoviewer-wrapper"><div class="css-z3e15g" data-testid="photoviewer-wrapper-hidden"></div><div data-testid="photoviewer-children" class="css-1a48zt4 ehw59r15"><figure class="css-jcw7oy e1g7ppur0" aria-label="media" role="group"><div class="css-1xdhyk6 erfvjey0"><span class="css-1ly73wi e1tej78p0">Image</span><div class="css-zjzyr8"><div data-testid="lazyimage-container" style="height:257.77777777777777px"></div></div></div><figcaption class="css-1l44abu ewdxa0s0"><span aria-hidden="true" class="css-16f3y1r e13ogyst0">Mr. Musk said he had been working up to 120 hours a week recently. “There were times when I didn’t leave the factory for three or four days — days when I didn’t go outside,” he said.</span><span class="css-cnj6d5 e1z0qqy90"><span class="css-1ly73wi e1tej78p0">Credit...</span><span>Joshua Lott/Getty Images</span></span></figcaption></figure></div></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">To help sleep when he is not working, Mr. Musk said he sometimes takes Ambien. “It is often a choice of no sleep or Ambien,” he said.</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div><div class="css-1fanzo5 StoryBodyCompanionColumn"><div class="css-53u6y8"><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">But this has worried some board members, who have noted that sometimes the drug does not put Mr. Musk to sleep but instead contributes to late-night Twitter sessions, according to a person familiar with the board’s thinking. Some board members are also aware that Mr. Musk has on occasion used recreational drugs, according to people familiar with the matter.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Tesla executives have been trying for years to recruit a chief operating officer or other No. 2 executive to assume some of Mr. Musk’s day-to-day responsibilities, according to people familiar with the matter. A couple of years ago, Mr. Musk said, the company approached Sheryl Sandberg, who is Facebook’s second-highest executive, about the job.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk said that “to the best of my knowledge,” there is “no active search right now.” But people familiar with the matter said a search is underway, and one person said it had intensified in the wake of Mr. Musk’s tweets.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0"><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">[The Tesla board “now must ask a sensitive but vital question,” </em><a class="css-1g7m0tk" href="http://web.archive.org/web/20201229193455/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/08/15/business/elon-musk-tesla-board.html" title=""><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">our columnist writes</em></a><em class="css-2fg4z9 e1gzwzxm0">. “What was Mr. Musk’s state of mind” when he tweeted?]</em></p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">In response to questions for this article, Tesla provided a statement that it attributed to its board, excluding Elon Musk. “There have been many false and irresponsible rumors in the press about the discussions of the Tesla board,” the statement said. “We would like to make clear that Elon’s commitment and dedication to Tesla is obvious. Over the past 15 years, Elon’s leadership of the Tesla team has caused Tesla to grow from a small start-up to having hundreds of thousands of cars on the road that customers love, employing tens of thousands of people around the world, and creating significant shareholder value in the process.”</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">Mr. Musk said he had no plans to relinquish his dual roles as chairman and chief executive.</p><p class="css-axufdj evys1bk0">But, he added, “if you have anyone who can do a better job, please let me know. They can have the job. Is there someone who can do the job better? They can have the reins right now.”</p></div><aside class="css-ew4tgv" aria-label="companion column"></aside></div></section><div class="bottom-of-article"></div><div></div><div></div><div><div and have angered some board members, according to people familiar with the matter. Efforts are underway to find a No. 2 executive to help take some of the pressure off Mr. Musk, people briefed on the search said. And some board members have expressed concern not only about Mr. Musk’s workload but also about his use of Ambien, two people familiar with the board said.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.12.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"For two decades, Mr. Musk has been one of Silicon Valley’s most brash and ambitious entrepreneurs, helping to found several influential technology companies. He has often carried himself with bravado, dismissing critics and relishing the spotlight that has come with his success and fortune. But in the interview, he demonstrated an extraordinary level of self-reflection and vulnerability, acknowledging that his myriad executive responsibilities are taking a steep personal toll.","formats":[]},"Video:VmlkZW86bnl0Oi8vdmlkZW8vNjM5MWQ0YzktYjE2NC01ZDMxLTgwMzUtODIzNzgxN2MyNzIy":{"id":"VmlkZW86bnl0Oi8vdmlkZW8vNjM5MWQ0YzktYjE2NC01ZDMxLTgwMzUtODIzNzgxN2MyNzIy","summary":"Serial entrepreneur Elon Musk wants to fundamentally change the way we live. But his path to success has been characterized by both great accomplishments and flirtations with failure.","firstPublished":"2018-08-18T19:55:38.000Z","aspectRatio":"16:9","transcript":"“I thought about what are the problems that are most likely to affect the future of the world or future of humanity.” Elon Musk, a South African-born engineer, inventor and billionaire, is one of the most important entrepreneurs in the world. But the 47-year-old is no stranger to turmoil, and by his own account his career has been full of highs and lows. “Just three years ago, I was showering at the Y and sleeping on the office floor. And now, I have got a million-dollar car and quite a few creature comforts.” After making a fortune with his first successful startup, Zip2, Musk gained fame as the co-founder of PayPal in the late 1990s. “So this is an ATM. What we’re going to do is transform the traditional banking industry.” The company lost hundreds of millions of dollars, and Musk was ousted as CEO because of internal turmoil. But when PayPal went public in 2002, and then was sold to eBay, Musk — as the biggest shareholder — walked away with $200 million. By the mid-2000s, Musk had turned around and invested almost all of his PayPal fortune into his new companies: SpaceX, Tesla and SolarCity. With SpaceX, Musk’s aspiration is out of this world — literally. It’s a commercial space flight company, which designs and launches advanced rockets into space. “Behind me is where Falcon 9 would sit during launch.” One of its ambitions is to eventually send people to Mars. “And I can’t think of anything more exciting than going out there and being among the stars.” But it has faced some roadblocks. Their first three rocket launches — failed, burning through almost all of the company’s assets. But SpaceX has had recent successes. The Falcon Heavy launch earlier this year was a big milestone and it carried an interesting payload: a dummy astronaut riding a Tesla car that was orbiting space. The company has also landed contracts with NASA, but questions about safety and cost remain. “We sold over 1,000 cars — and by sold I mean people put down a substantial deposit on the car.” Tesla, Musk’s electric car company, is one of the first of its kind to work on a large scale. It has also successfully disrupted the auto industry. The company is valued at over $50 billion. The Tesla Model S has been called the greatest car ever built. But Tesla has also faced some setbacks, including car batteries that caught on fire, recalls and production delays. SolarCity, Musk’s renewable energy venture, initially had a bright future. It’s the largest provider of rooftop solar systems in the U.S., and the company boomed on Wall Street. But after investors raised doubts about its business model, SolarCity’s shares quickly lost value, and Tesla had to take over in what was widely seen as a bailout. “We really need to make solar panels as appealing as electric cars have become.” But while Musk may have been flirting with bankruptcy and calamity for years, his projects continue to captivate the collective imagination. “Life has to be more than about solving problems. Musk.]","formats":[{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.18.content.2.formats.0","typename":"ItalicFormat"}]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.18.content.2.formats.0":{"__typename":"ItalicFormat","type":null},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.19.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"That morning, Mr. Musk woke up at home with his girlfriend, the musician known as Grimes, and had an early workout. Then he got in a Tesla Model S and drove himself to the airport. En route, Mr. Musk typed his fateful message.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.23.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Musk has said he saw the tweet as an attempt at transparency. He acknowledged Thursday that no one had seen or reviewed it before he posted it.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.25.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Tesla’s shares soared. Investors, analysts and journalists puzzled over the tweet — published in the middle of the day’s official market trading, an unusual time to release major news — including the price Mr. Musk cited. He said in the interview that he wanted to offer a roughly 20 percent premium over where the stock had been recently trading, which would have been about $419. He decided to round up to $420 — a number that has become code for marijuana in counterculture lore.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.27.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“It seemed like better karma at $420 than at $419,” he said in the interview. “But I was not on weed, to be clear. Weed is not helpful for productivity. There’s a reason for the word ‘stoned.’ You just sit there like a stone on weed.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.29.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Musk reached the airport and flew on a private plane to Nevada, where he spent the day visiting a Tesla battery plant known as the Gigafactory, including time meeting with managers and working on an assembly line. That evening, he flew to the San Francisco Bay Area, where he held Tesla meetings late into the night.","formats":[]},"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvNDFhN2MxZTctY2I5My01ZjkwLTgxN2YtYzhmZTU3YTA2NmI1":{"id":"SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvNDFhN2MxZTctY2I5My01ZjkwLTgxN2YtYzhmZTU3YTA2NmI1","imageType":"photo","url":"\u002Fimagepages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F16\u002Fbusiness\u002F17MUSK05.html","uri":"nyt:\u002F\u002Fimage\u002F41a7c1e7-cb93-5f90-817f-c8fe57a066b5","credit":"David Paul Morris\u002FBloomberg","legacyHtmlCaption":"Mr. Musk declared on Twitter last week that he hoped to convert the publicly traded company into a private one. Tesla’s shares soared afterward.","crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]})":[{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvNDFhN2MxZTctY2I5My01ZjkwLTgxN2YtYzhmZTU3YTA2NmI1.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).0","typename":"ImageCrop"},{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvNDFhN2MxZTctY2I5My01ZjkwLTgxN2YtYzhmZTU3YTA2NmI1.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).1","typename":"ImageCrop"}],"caption":{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"$Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvNDFhN2MxZTctY2I5My01ZjkwLTgxN2YtYzhmZTU3YTA2NmI1.caption","typename":"TextOnlyDocumentBlock"},"__typename":"Image"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-articleLarge.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17MUSK05\u002Fmerlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-articleLarge.jpg","name":"articleLarge","width":600,"height":400,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-popup.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17MUSK05\u002Fmerlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-popup.jpg","name":"popup","width":650,"height":433,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-jumbo.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17MUSK05\u002Fmerlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-jumbo.jpg","name":"jumbo","width":1024,"height":683,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-superJumbo.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17MUSK05\u002Fmerlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-superJumbo.jpg","name":"superJumbo","width":2048,"height":1366,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvNDFhN2MxZTctY2I5My01ZjkwLTgxN2YtYzhmZTU3YTA2NmI1.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).0":{"renditions":[{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-articleLarge.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"},{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-popup.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"},{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-jumbo.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"},{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-superJumbo.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"}],"__typename":"ImageCrop"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-articleInline.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17MUSK05\u002Fmerlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-articleInline.jpg","name":"articleInline","width":190,"height":127,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvNDFhN2MxZTctY2I5My01ZjkwLTgxN2YtYzhmZTU3YTA2NmI1.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).1":{"renditions":[{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17MUSK05merlin_142442715_99e05cd0-a475-4afc-ad26-0c751483d712-articleInline.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"}],"__typename":"ImageCrop"},"$Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvNDFhN2MxZTctY2I5My01ZjkwLTgxN2YtYzhmZTU3YTA2NmI1.caption":{"text":"Mr. Musk declared on Twitter last week that he hoped to convert the publicly traded company into a private one. Tesla’s shares soared afterward.","__typename":"TextOnlyDocumentBlock"},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.33.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"What Mr. Musk meant by “funding secured” has become an important question. Those two words helped propel Tesla’s shares higher.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.35.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"But that funding, it turned out, was far from secure.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.36.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Musk has said he was referring to a potential investment by Saudi Arabia’s government investment fund. Mr. Musk had extensive talks with representatives of the $250 billion fund about possibly financing a transaction to take Tesla private — maybe even in a manner that would have resulted in the Saudis’ owning most of the company. One of those sessions took place on July 31 at the Tesla factory in the Bay Area, according to a person familiar with the meeting. But the Saudi fund had not committed to provide any cash, two people briefed on the discussions said.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.38.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Another possibility under consideration is that SpaceX, Mr. Musk’s rocket company, would help bankroll the Tesla privatization and would take an ownership stake in the carmaker, according to people familiar with the matter.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.40.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Musk’s tweet kicked off a chain reaction.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.41.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"An hour and 20 minutes after the tweet, with Tesla’s shares up 7 percent, the Nasdaq stock exchange halted trading, and Tesla published a letter to employees from Mr. Musk explaining the rationale for possibly taking the company private. When the shares resumed trading, they continued their climb, ending the day with an 11 percent gain.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.43.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"The next day, investigators in the San Francisco office of the Securities and Exchange Commission asked Tesla for explanations. Ordinarily, such material information about a public company’s plans is laid out in detail after extensive internal preparation and issued through official channels. Board members, blindsided by the chief executive’s market-moving statement, were angry that they had not been briefed, two people familiar with the matter said. They scrambled to cobble together a public statement trying to defuse a mounting uproar over the seemingly haphazard communication.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.45.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Musk said in the interview that board members had not complained to him about his tweet. “I don’t recall getting any communications from the board at all,” he said. “I definitely did not get calls from irate directors.”","formats":[]},"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvYTA3Y2M5NDQtMDljZi01YjYyLWJmYTUtMDg3OGNlNGE3ZjNj":{"id":"SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvYTA3Y2M5NDQtMDljZi01YjYyLWJmYTUtMDg3OGNlNGE3ZjNj","imageType":"photo","url":"\u002Fimagepages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F16\u002Fbusiness\u002F17musk-2-print.html","uri":"nyt:\u002F\u002Fimage\u002Fa07cc944-09cf-5b62-bfa5-0878ce4a7f3c","credit":"Justin Kaneps for The New York Times","legacyHtmlCaption":"The Model 3 assembly line at Tesla&rsquo;s factory in Fremont, Calif. The company has been racing to meet production targets on the new model.","crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]})":[{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvYTA3Y2M5NDQtMDljZi01YjYyLWJmYTUtMDg3OGNlNGE3ZjNj.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).0","typename":"ImageCrop"},{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvYTA3Y2M5NDQtMDljZi01YjYyLWJmYTUtMDg3OGNlNGE3ZjNj.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).1","typename":"ImageCrop"}],"caption":{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"$Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvYTA3Y2M5NDQtMDljZi01YjYyLWJmYTUtMDg3OGNlNGE3ZjNj.caption","typename":"TextOnlyDocumentBlock"},"__typename":"Image"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-articleLarge.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17musk-2-print\u002Fmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-articleLarge.jpg","name":"articleLarge","width":600,"height":464,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-popup.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17musk-2-print\u002Fmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-popup.jpg","name":"popup","width":648,"height":500,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-jumbo.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17musk-2-print\u002Fmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-jumbo.jpg","name":"jumbo","width":1024,"height":791,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-superJumbo.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17musk-2-print\u002Fmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-superJumbo.jpg","name":"superJumbo","width":2048,"height":1582,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvYTA3Y2M5NDQtMDljZi01YjYyLWJmYTUtMDg3OGNlNGE3ZjNj.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).0":{"renditions":[{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-articleLarge.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"},{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-popup.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"},{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-jumbo.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"},{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-superJumbo.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"}],"__typename":"ImageCrop"},"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-articleInline.jpg":{"url":"http:\u002F\u002Fweb.archive.org\u002Fweb\u002F20201229193455\u002Fhttps:\u002F\u002Fstatic01.nyt.com\u002Fimages\u002F2018\u002F08\u002F17\u002Fbusiness\u002F17musk-2-print\u002Fmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-articleInline.jpg","name":"articleInline","width":190,"height":147,"__typename":"ImageRendition"},"Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvYTA3Y2M5NDQtMDljZi01YjYyLWJmYTUtMDg3OGNlNGE3ZjNj.crops({\"renditionNames\":[\"articleLarge\",\"jumbo\",\"superJumbo\",\"articleInline\",\"popup\",\"mobileMasterAt3x\"]}).1":{"renditions":[{"type":"id","generated":false,"id":"ImageRendition:images20180817business17musk-2-printmerlin_141700365_40647f7f-cf94-41bc-b647-969886e1e084-articleInline.jpg","typename":"ImageRendition"}],"__typename":"ImageCrop"},"$Image:SW1hZ2U6bnl0Oi8vaW1hZ2UvYTA3Y2M5NDQtMDljZi01YjYyLWJmYTUtMDg3OGNlNGE3ZjNj.caption":{"text":"The Model 3 assembly line at Tesla&rsquo;s factory in Fremont, Calif. The company has been racing to meet production targets on the new model.","__typename":"TextOnlyDocumentBlock"},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.49.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"But shortly after the Times published its interview with Mr. Musk, he added through a Tesla spokeswoman that Antonio Gracias, Tesla’s lead independent director, had indeed contacted him to discuss the Aug. 7 Twitter post, and that he had agreed not to tweet again about the possible privatization deal unless he had discussed it with the board.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.51.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"In the interview, Mr. Musk added that he did not regret his Twitter post — “Why would I?” — and said he had no plans to stop using the social media platform. Some board members, however, have recently told Mr. Musk that he should lay off Twitter and focus on making cars and launching rockets, according to people familiar with the matter.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.53.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"[","formats":[{"type":"id","generated":true,"id":"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.53.content.0.formats.0","typename":"ItalicFormat"}]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.53.content.0.formats.0":{"__typename":"ItalicFormat","type":null},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.53.content.1":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Kara S.E.C. investigation appears to be intensifying rapidly. Just days after the agency’s request for information, Tesla’s board and Mr. Musk received S.E.C. subpoenas, according to a person familiar with the matter. Board members and Mr. Musk are preparing to meet with S.E.C. officials as soon as next week, the person said.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.57.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"In the interview on Thursday, Mr. Musk alternated between laughter and tears.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.58.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"He said he had been working up to 120 hours a week recently — echoing the reason he cited in a recent public apology to an analyst whom he had berated. SpaceX, Mr. Musk’s rocket company, is said to be a possible source of funding for the Tesla privatization.","__typename":"TextOnlyDocumentBlock"},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.64.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Musk stopped talking, seemingly overcome by emotion.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.65.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"He turned 47 on June 28, and he said he spent the full 24 hours of his birthday at work. “All night — no friends, nothing,” he said, struggling to get the words out.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.67.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Two days later, he was scheduled to be the best man at the wedding of his brother, Kimbal, in Catalonia. Mr. Musk said he flew directly there from the factory, arriving just two hours before the ceremony. Immediately afterward, he got back on the plane and returned straight to Tesla headquarters, where work on the mass-market Model 3 has been all consuming.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.69.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Musk paused again.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.70.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"“I thought the worst of it was over — I thought it was,” he said. “The worst is over from a Tesla operational standpoint.” He continued: “But from a personal pain standpoint, the worst is yet to come.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.72.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"He blamed short-sellers — investors who bet that Tesla’s shares will lose value — for much of his stress. said.","__typename":"TextOnlyDocumentBlock"},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.81.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"To said.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.83.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"But this has worried some board members, who have noted that sometimes the drug does not put Mr. Musk to sleep but instead contributes to late-night Twitter sessions, according to a person familiar with the board’s thinking. Some board members are also aware that Mr. Musk has on occasion used recreational drugs, according to people familiar with the matter.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.85.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Tesla executives have been trying for years to recruit a chief operating officer or other No. 2 executive to assume some of Mr. Musk’s day-to-day responsibilities, according to people familiar with the matter. A couple of years ago, Mr. Musk said, the company approached Sheryl Sandberg, who is Facebook’s second-highest executive, about the job.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.87.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Musk said that “to the best of my knowledge,” there is “no active search right now.” But people familiar with the matter said a search is underway, and one person said it had intensified in the wake of Mr. Musk’s tweets.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.89.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"[The response to questions for this article, Tesla provided a statement that it attributed to its board, excluding Elon Musk. “There have been many false and irresponsible rumors in the press about the discussions of the Tesla board,” the statement said. “We would like to make clear that Elon’s commitment and dedication to Tesla is obvious. Over the past 15 years, Elon’s leadership of the Tesla team has caused Tesla to grow from a small start-up to having hundreds of thousands of cars on the road that customers love, employing tens of thousands of people around the world, and creating significant shareholder value in the process.”","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.93.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"Mr. Musk said he had no plans to relinquish his dual roles as chairman and chief executive.","formats":[]},"$Article:QXJ0aWNsZTpueXQ6Ly9hcnRpY2xlLzk0NzEyMTgxLTY4ZjAtNTdjMy1hN2I3LWRiMDNkYzhmZTdkYw==.sprinkledBody.content@filterEmpty.94.content.0":{"__typename":"TextInline","text":"But, he added, “if you have anyone who can do a better job, please let me know. They can have the reins right
